title: Flights & Flatbread with Kosta Browne Winery
description:
Join us on Tuesday, April 21st from 4–8 PM at Whispering Vine Fourth Street for a special Flights & Flatbread evening featuring the acclaimed wines of Kosta Browne Winery, one of Sonoma County’s most celebrated producers of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay. Known for its cult status and named Wine Spectator’s #1 Wine of the Year in 2011, Kosta Browne is revered for crafting layered, site-driven wines of exceptional character and finesse.

Tasting Fee: $35 (Includes a flatbread and a tasting of four Kosta Browne wines)

If you buy a bottle, the tasting fee will be waived.

Reserve your Spot

Flatbread:

Mushroom, leeks, fennel, and fontina sausage flatbread.

Featured Wines:

• Kosta Browne Barrel Auction Pinot Noir 2021 – Only 120 bottles made – A rare, one-time blend of premium single vineyards delivering depth, complexity, and true collector appeal.

• Kosta Browne Beaune Premier Cru Pinot Noir 2020 – 94 pts James Suckling – Juicy and layered with dark fruit, iron, spice, flowers, earth, and black pepper.

• Kosta Browne Sta. Rita Hills Pinot Noir 2021 – 94 pts Wine Enthusiast, 93 pts James Suckling, 93 pts Wine Advocate– Expressive and polished with strawberry, cherry compote, spice, tea, and a long, savory finish.

• Kosta Browne Sonoma Coast Pinot Noir 2021 – 94 pts James Suckling, 93 pts Wine Advocate – Concentrated and elegant with red cherry, cranberry, tea leaf, lavender, and a spicy, seamless finish.

Enjoy an evening of world-class Pinot Noir, perfectly paired flatbread, and one of California’s most iconic winery names.
